No. 4 has been duly providing. 
(b) Respondent No. 4 has never manipulated or coerced his son, 
Aadith, who desired to go to India and stay with his father. 
Furthermore, after the order dated 16.12.2024 was read over 
to Aadith, he expressed that he did not want to reside with his 
406 
mother, even though he had been interacting with her frequently 
on video call. In fact, when he met his mother on 24.12.2024, 
he was visibly distressed by the Appellant drudging up old 
memories to convince him to live with her. He also expressed 
that he wanted to be represented in the proceedings before this 
Court. Having sought legal consultation, Respondent No. 4 was 
informed that as Aadith was a major, he had the right to consult 
and appoint his own lawyers. Accordingly, Aadith interacted with 
a lawyer in Delhi over calls and provided instructions to file the 
interlocutory applications before this Court. The decision not 
to live with the Appellant was solely taken by Aadith, out of his
